title: scaevola tenuifolia
intro: Scaevola tenuifolia, also known as blue fan flower, is a flowering plant native to Australia. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of iridoid glycosides and flavonoids. The plant is believed to provide various health benefits, including relief from fever, sore throats, and skin conditions, as well as acting as an antiseptic. Scaevola tenuifolia is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments.
